answer:
The function is \( f(t) = e^{-2t} u(t) \). At \( t = 0^+ \), \( f(0^+) = e^0 = 1 \), and the derivative is \( f'(t) = -2e^{-2t} \), so the slope at \( 0^+ \) is \(-2\). The initial-value theorem also gives \( f(0^+) = \lim_{s \to \infty} sF(s) = \lim_{s \to \infty} \frac{s}{s+2} = 1 \), and the slope from the theorem matches \(-2\).  

\boxed{J}
